mainEntity.name: How much does a Discovery engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Discovery engine typically costs £2,500–£9,500 supply only, depending on generation and engine [EM-QUOTE]. Tdi-era units sit at £2,500–£4,500; the 3.0 diesels at £6,500–£9,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,900. Use our Discovery calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Discovery engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, the 300Tdi — mechanically injected and endlessly rebuildable. In the modern range, the AJ20D6 D300 (2021+) is proving the most dependable, followed by post-2019 Ingenium four-cylinders. (Note: the 4.4 SDV8 often cited as JLR's most reliable engine was never fitted to a Discovery.)

mainEntity.name: Which Discovery engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2.7 TDV6 (276DT) and 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (306DT) carry the highest catastrophic-failure risk — oil pump failure and crankshaft bearing failure respectively, both capable of leaving nothing to rebuild. Neither is a reason to walk away from a documented car; both are reasons to walk away from an undocumented one.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my Discovery engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Warning signs by engine: cold-start knock (306DT), sudden oil-pressure loss (276DT), cold-start chain rattle (pre-2019 204DTD), blue smoke or excessive oil consumption (any). Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery 4 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the car's value. A Discovery 4 worth £10,000–£18,000 justifies a £6,500–£9,500 reconditioned SDV6; a £6,000 car sits at our 60% scrap threshold [EM-QUOTE]. Use the Ownership Decision Tool for a personalised verdict.

mainEntity.name: Which Discovery generation should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Discovery 5 (2021+) for the best all-round profile on the AJ20D6. Discovery 4 SDV6 for value — only with proven oil-interval history. Avoid any Discovery 3 or early 4 on the 2.7 TDV6 without full service history.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Stamped on the engine block (typically the left side) and listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation — or tell us your variant (TDV6, SDV6, D300 etc.) and we'll identify it.

mainEntity.name: Discovery 4 vs Discovery 5 — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Discovery 5, particularly from 2021 on the AJ20D6 six. Discovery 4 is cheaper to buy and more capable off-road out of the box, but the SDV6 carries real crankshaft-bearing risk at higher mileages. Service history matters more than the badge.

hero.tagPill: Discovery • 1989–Present • 5 Generations • Tdi, TD5, TDV6, SDV6, Ingenium
hero.h1: Discovery Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every generation. Every engine code. Every known failure. Every replacement cost. From the 200Tdi original to the Ingenium-six D300 — backed by 2,800+ real UK enquiries across Discovery-fitted engines [EM-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ Engine-Dependent — Discovery 5 and well-priced Discovery 4s justify replacement; a £6,500–£9,500 SDV6 recon against a £6,000 Discovery 4 sits at our scrap threshold [EM-QUOTE]
★★★☆☆ — excellent bookends (Tdi era, post-2021 D5), real risk in the middle (2.7/3.0 V6 diesels)
2.7 TDV6 (276DT) — oil pump failure • 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (306DT) — crankshaft bearing failure at high mileage
Discovery 4 SDV6 with proven oil-interval history — our homepage 'Best Used Buy' ✅
Discovery 5 D300 (2021+) — revised Ingenium six, no V6 crank legacy
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Best Family Land Rover (Discovery 5) • Discovery 4 SDV6 = Best Used Buy

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The Discovery is the best family Land Rover ever made and the model where engine choice matters most — the difference between the right and wrong engine is the difference between a £500 service bill and a £9,500 crankshaft.

generations.subHeadline: Five generations, five very different ownership propositions. Select yours to explore engines, problems, and costs.

cards.title: Discovery 1 (D1)
cards.verdict: The honest original. The Tdi engines are effectively immortal — the body and chassis rust long before the engine tires. Buy on structure, not mileage.

cards.title: Discovery 2 (D2)
cards.verdict: The TD5 is a strong, well-understood engine with cheap known fixes. The vehicle around it — rear chassis rot, ACE suspension — is the real buying risk.

cards.title: Discovery 3 (L319)
cards.verdict: A generational leap in capability carrying the range's most sudden failure mode — 276DT oil pump failure gives little warning. Full service history or walk away.

cards.title: Discovery 4 (L319)
cards.verdict: Best value in the range if maintained correctly. The SDV6 crank-bearing risk is real at higher mileages — proven oil-interval history is the whole game.

cards.title: Discovery 5 (L462)
cards.verdict: Safest modern choice and our Best Family Land Rover. Prefer 2021+ builds: the AJ20D6 six replaces both the old V6 and the early four-cylinder concerns in one move.

engineDatabase.h2: Discovery Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the UK Discovery, with reliability ratings, real enquiry volumes, and replacement costs.

engines.enquiries: — (aggregated under 306DT in source data — split before citing)

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: 276DT, 306DT and 204DTD ratings: 94% confidence — the three highest-volume engines in our entire dataset [EM-VERIFIED], cross-referenced with specialist rebuild reports. D1/D2-era and petrol V8 ratings: Expert-knowledge tier — documented failure patterns and specialist interviews, not proprietary volume data. Data note (internal): Enquiry volumes from EM_Proprietary_Data_2025.txt [EM-VERIFIED]; costs from Table A [EM-QUOTE]. Correction applied: the original Discovery strategy sheet listed 368DT (3.6 TDV8) and 508PS (4.4 SDV8) as Discovery engines — neither was ever fitted to a Discovery (they belong to Range Rover / Range Rover Sport per our own blueprint mapping). Removed. Correction applied: post-2021 D5 D250/D300 is the AJ20D6 Ingenium six, not the 306DT V6.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The failures Discovery owners actually report — with symptoms, costs, and urgency levels.

problems.description: The 3.0 TDV6/SDV6's defining weak point. Cold-start knock is the early warning; once bearing failure starts there's rarely anything left to rebuild. £6,500–£9,500 recon [EM-QUOTE]. Affects Discovery 4 and 2017–2020 Discovery 5.
Often kills the 2.7 TDV6 outright before any other symptom appears — the most sudden failure mode in our data. Affects Discovery 3 and early Discovery 4.
Pre-2019 Ingenium diesel tensioner wear; cold-start rattle is the warning. Significantly improved from 2019. Affects 2017–2019 Discovery 5.
Common across Discovery 3, 4 and 5; compressor burnout presents a fire risk. Not an engine failure, but the most common non-engine enquiry we receive on this model.
Oil wicks up the injector loom into the ECU connector — rough running misdiagnosed as ECU failure. Cheap fix caught early. Affects Discovery 2.
Short-journey D5 diesels can suffer fuel-in-oil dilution from interrupted DPF regens — accelerates wear if oil changes are stretched.

marketIntelligence.h2: Discovery Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.data: 1,150 [EM-VERIFIED] — the single most-enquired engine in our dataset
signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising [PLACEHOLDER — insert YoY % from source data]
⬇ Falling as D3 parc shrinks [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The three highest-volume engines in our entire 2025 dataset — 204DTD, 306DT, 276DT — were all fitted to the Discovery. No other model sits at the centre of UK engine-replacement demand like this one.

Discovery 4 generates the most failure-driven enquiries in the range; Discovery 5 enquiries skew toward pre-purchase checks — the same reliability signature we see on the Defender L663. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)
276DT demand is declining as Discovery 3 values fall below our scrap threshold — an early signal that D3 ownership economics are closing. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every Discovery Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one Discovery based on this page, buy a 2021+ Discovery 5 D300 — or a Discovery 4 SDV6 whose oil-change invoices you can hold in your hand. On this model, paperwork is worth more than mileage.

replacementCosts.h2: Discovery Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery engine typically takes 10–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].

economicsBox.title: The Discovery rule
economicsBox.text: Unlike the Defender, replacement is not automatically safe here. A Discovery 4 SDV6 worth £6,000–£18,000 against a £6,500–£9,500 recon sits at ⚠️ Watch on our 60% scrap-threshold rule — the cheapest cars in that band fail it. Discovery 3s on the 2.7 TDV6 increasingly fall below the threshold outright. Get a scrap valuation alongside any repair quote before committing.

engineEvolution.h2: How the Discovery Engine Range Evolved — 1989 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: The Discovery existed to put Range Rover capability at family prices — the Tdi diesels made that economically real
eras.worthKnowing: Same immortal engines as the Defender; the Discovery body rusts around them

eras.whyBmwChanged: Emissions rules demanded electronic management — the TD5 was Land Rover's answer, and its last fully in-house engine
eras.worthKnowing: Shared with the Defender; parts and knowledge are everywhere

eras.whyBmwChanged: Ford ownership brought the jointly developed 'Lion' V6 diesels — huge refinement gains, new failure modes
eras.worthKnowing: The 276DT oil pump and 306DT crank bearing define this era's ownership risk

eras.whyBmwChanged: JLR independence meant in-house modular engines — the four-cylinder Ingenium arrived while the old V6 soldiered on
eras.worthKnowing: Pre-2019 204DTD carries the timing-chain caveat; the V6 kept its crank one

eras.whyBmwChanged: One engine family to replace both problem lineages — the straight-six retired the Ford V6 and its bearing legacy in a single stroke
eras.worthKnowing: The cleanest engine slate a Discovery has had since the 300Tdi

profiles.verdict: The best seven-seat 4x4 ownership proposition in the range — our homepage's Best Family Land Rover. Discovery 5 first, well-documented Discovery 4 second.
D5 P300 or D300 work well; avoid short-run diesel commuting (DPF/oil dilution). A Discovery 4 as a short-hop commuter is how crank bearings die.
3,500kg capacity across modern generations; the SDV6 and D300 are exceptional tow engines. Britain's caravan-club default for a reason.
The trap tier. Cheap Discovery 3s and 4s carry engine risk that can exceed the car's value. If the budget can't absorb a £6,500 engine, buy a TD5-era car or don't buy a Discovery.
A 2021+ D300 or a cherished Tdi are genuine keepers. The middle generations are keepers only with disciplined maintenance.
